title: Number of one-year-olds who are not vaccinated against measles
caption:
Number of one-year-olds who are not vaccinated against measles

Measles is a highly contagious viral disease, most common in young children. Its effects include blindness, inflammation of the brain, severe diarrhea, and severe respiratory infections such as pneumonia.

This choropleth map displays the estimated number of one-year-olds who did not receive the measles vaccine in 2023, using a color scale ranging from light beige for zero unvaccinated children to dark red for one million. The highest concentrations of unvaccinated children, represented by the darkest red bands indicating values between 300,000 and 1 million, are visible in India, Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Pakistan, and Ethiopia. A second tier of countries, shaded in medium-dark red corresponding to the 100,000 to 300,000 range, includes Indonesia, the Philippines, Brazil, Mexico, and China. Several nations in South America, such as Colombia and Peru, along with countries in Southeast Asia and parts of Eastern Europe, fall into the 30,000 to 100,000 category. Lighter orange shades, representing 10,000 to 30,000 unvaccinated children, cover much of the United States, Canada, Australia, and various nations across Africa and Asia. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data is available. The visual distribution shows that the largest absolute numbers of unvaccinated one-year-olds are clustered in specific regions of South Asia and Sub-Saharan Africa, while many high-income nations appear in the lower value bands.
